steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: After "investigate how," the blank must complete an indirect question — a subordinate noun clause that acts as the object of the verb. Indirect questions use statement word order (subject before auxiliary verb) and end with a period, not a question mark.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — could their use be better regulated?: flips auxiliary before subject, treating this as a direct question. Indirect questions never use direct-question word order. ✗
  • Choice B — their use could be better regulated.: keeps subject before auxiliary ("their use could be better regulated") and ends with a period. Correct indirect question structure. ✓
  • Choice C — their use could be better regulated?: keeps statement word order, but the question mark incorrectly ends a declarative sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — could their use be better regulated.: uses inverted direct-question word order, which is wrong inside an indirect question, even though it ends with a period. ✗
That is the correct answer.
